name: Premier League
description: English top-flight soccer — clubs, grounds, derbies, and legends

questions.q: Anfield is the home ground of which Premier League club?

source.notes: Anfield has been Liverpool's home since 1892.

questions.q: Which club plays its home matches at Old Trafford?

source.notes: Nicknamed 'The Theatre of Dreams'; opened 1910.

questions.q: What is the nickname of Arsenal Football Club?

source.notes: From the club's origins at the Woolwich Arsenal munitions factory; a cannon appears on the crest.

questions.q: Manchester United are traditionally known by which red-themed nickname?

source.notes: Adopted by manager Matt Busby in the 1960s, inspired by Salford's rugby club.

questions.q: The FA Cup Final is traditionally played at which stadium?

source.notes: Original Wembley 1923–2000; new Wembley opened 2007.

questions.q: How many teams are relegated from the Premier League at the end of each season?

source.notes: Bottom three drop to the Championship; three are promoted up.

questions.q: In football officiating, what does the abbreviation VAR stand for?

source.notes: Introduced to the Premier League in 2019.

questions.q: Stamford Bridge has been the home ground of which West London club since 1905?

source.notes: The club was founded specifically to play at the existing Stamford Bridge stadium.

questions.q: The Etihad Stadium is the home ground of which Premier League side?

source.notes: Moved in from Maine Road in 2003; originally built for the 2002 Commonwealth Games.

questions.q: Villa Park in Birmingham is the home ground of which Premier League club?

source.notes: Villa have played there since 1897.

questions.q: A lion rampant appears on the club crest of which London Premier League side?

source.notes: Chelsea's crest features a lion holding a staff, taken from the coat of arms of a former club president.

questions.q: Leicester City famously won their first Premier League title in which year after starting at 5000-to-1 odds?

source.notes: Claudio Ranieri's Foxes won the 2015–16 Premier League title.

questions.q: Who is the all-time leading goalscorer in Premier League history with 260 goals?

source.notes: Shearer scored 260 PL goals (1992–2006) for Blackburn and Newcastle; still the record as of 2026.

questions.q: Thierry Henry is the all-time leading goalscorer for which English club?

source.notes: 228 goals for Arsenal across two spells (1999–2007, 2012).

questions.q: David Beckham left which English club in 2003 to sign for Real Madrid?

source.notes: Transferred to Real Madrid in summer 2003 for around £25m.

questions.q: The North London Derby is contested between which two Premier League clubs?

source.notes: Rival clubs separated by about 4 miles in north London.

questions.q: The Merseyside Derby is contested between Liverpool and which other club?

source.notes: Known as the 'friendly derby' but one of the most-red-carded fixtures in PL history.

questions.q: Sir Alex Ferguson, who managed Manchester United for 26 years, is from which country?

source.notes: Born in Govan, Glasgow in 1941.

questions.q: Selhurst Park is the home ground of which Premier League club?

source.notes: Palace have played at Selhurst Park since 1924.

questions.q: The Tyne-Wear Derby is played between Newcastle United and which Premier League-or-Championship rival?

source.notes: One of the oldest and most intense rivalries in English football.

questions.q: Which north London stadium was Arsenal's home ground until they moved out in 2006?

source.notes: Arsenal Stadium (Highbury) 1913–2006; replaced by the Emirates Stadium.

questions.q: Kenny Dalglish, as player-manager, guided which club to its only Premier League title in 1995?

source.notes: Rovers won the 1994–95 Premier League title — their last top-flight championship.
